TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA) ** AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ISSUES AN ORDER 1) DECLARING PATRINA HARRISON A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT 2) PREFILING ORDER TO PATRINA HARRISON AND 3) CONTINUING HEARING ON MOTION TO REQUIRE SECURITY. HEARING ON MOTION TO REQUIRE SECURITY IS SET FOR APRIL 23, 2025 AT 9:00 A.M. DEPARTMENT 301. WHOLE FOODS MAY SUBMIT ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE, AND BRIEFING OF NO MORE THAN 10 PAGES, IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION NO LATER THAN MARCH 19, 2025; HARRISON MAY SUBMIT EVIDENCE, AND BRIEFING OF NO MORE THAN 10 PAGES, IN SUPPORT OF HER OPPOSITION BY NO LATER THAT APRIL 16, 2025. **

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO AUG-04-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON'S MOTION FOR WAIVER OF SECURITY (BOND) FOR INDIGENT VEXATIOUS LITIGANT PATRINA HARRISON; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THIS COURT PREVIOUSLY ORDERED PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON TO POST A SECURITY BOND TO PROCEED WITH HER ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC. HARRISON NOW MOVES THE COURT TO WAIVE THE EFFECT OF ITS ORDER IN LIGHT OF HER INDIGENCE. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR THE REASONS STATED HEREIN (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL DAVID ADAMS IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: JANE HONG, CSR# 11975 (TEL: 909-831-2644 / EMAIL: CSRJANE@HOTMAIL.COM) (301/CVA)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-22-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WHOLE FOOD MARKET CALIFORNIA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST THROUGH FIFTH, SEVENTH THROUGH NINTH AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH, FIFTH, SEVENTH, EIGHTH, NINTH AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT AN ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-22-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT; MATTER ARGUED AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. UPON CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE COURT FINDS THE UNRUH ACT DOES NOT ALLOW FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THEREFORE, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT STATES THE ISSUE CAN BE REVISITED CONTINGENT UPON PLAINTIFF PROVIDING AUTHORITY TO THE CONTRARY. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS OTHERWISE ADOPTED. COUNSEL TROY HEISMAN IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH CONFORMS TO THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Waiver Of Security (Bond) For Indigent Vexatious Litigant Patrina Harrison; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Patrina Harrison
Off Calendar Aug-04-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. David Adams, Esq. of Blaxter | Blackman LLP (tel: 415-500-7709) for the Defendant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON's Motion For Waiver Of Security (Bond) For Indigent Vexatious Litigant Patrina Harrison.

The Court notes Plaintiff Patrina Harrison contested the tentative ruling. The Court notes the Plaintiff is not present in Court as of 9:22 A.M. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

This court previously ordered plaintiff Patrina Harrison to post a security bond to proceed with her action against defendant Whole Foods Market California Inc. Harrison now moves the court to waive the effect of its order in light of her indigence. The court denies the motion for the reasons stated herein.

Harrison recites that she cannot afford the bond and would like to proceed with this case nonetheless. This is an issue she raised in her opposition to Whole Foods' motion to require her to post security. This untimely motion to reconsider presents no facts or circumstances that were not available to Harris to present in her opposition. Even considering this as a new motion instead of a motion to reconsider, the court finds the motion without merit.

An important legislative purpose underlying the vexatious litigant statutes is to "minimize the number of frivolous filings." (Devereaux v. Latham & Watkins (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1571, 1582 [disapproved on other grounds by Moran v. Murtaugh Miller Meyer & Nelson, LLP (2007) 40 Cal.4th 780].) The Legislature was likely aware that some vexatious litigants would be unable to post security but did not expressly include a means or indigency test in CCP 391.1(a), the security statute. Indeed, the Devereaux court noted the Legislature considered this issue. (Id. at p. 1587-1588.) Thus, courts in exercising their discretion over the amount of an undertaking should look at the defendant's probable cost of defending the action, but are not required to consider a vexatious litigant's ability to post the bond. (Devereaux, supra, 32 Cal.App.4th at p. 1588; see also McColm v. Westwood Park Ass'n (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 1211, 1219 disapproved on other grounds by John v. Superior Court (2016) 63 Cal.4th 91].)

Plaintiff also challenges the constitutionality of the vexatious litigant statutes as applied to indigent litigants, but this argument has been considered and rejected by the appellate courts. (See, e.g., Moran, supra, 40 Cal.4th at p. 786.)

Counsel David Adams is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR# 11975 (tel: 909-831-2644 / email: csrjane@hotmail.com) (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Continued To Apr-09-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff PATRINA HARRISON in pro per present (tel: 415-567-8182).
Atty. TROY HEISMAN, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Blaxter|Blackman LLP (tel: 415-500-7700) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC.'s DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Whole Food Market California, Inc.'s demurrer to the first through fifth, seventh through ninth and twelfth causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled as to the first, second, fourth, fifth, seventh, eighth, ninth and twelfth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The parties' requests to take judicial notice of the contents of the police report are denied. The contents of the police report are not admissible because the police report contains multiple levels of inadmissible hearsay. Nor are the contents of the police report judicially noticeable since the police report includes matters that are reasonably disputable and not easily verifiable. Since the contents of the police report are not judicially noticeable, all grounds for the demurrer relying on the contents of the police report lack merit. Moreover, plaintiff Patricia Harrison adequately alleges that she was threatened and harmed because of her race, thereby defeating the additional grounds for the demurrer to the fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action. Because Ms. Harrison adequately alleges pre-suit notice, the first ground for the demurrer to the eighth cause of action lacks merit. Because Ms. Harrison adequately alleges conduct prohibited by the CLRA and UCL, the second ground for the demurrer to the eighth cause of action and the sole ground for the demurrer to the ninth cause of action lack merit. Because respondeat superior is not a cause of action, the demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

Prevailing party is to prepare and submit an order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff PATRINA HARRISON in pro per present (tel: 415-567-8182).
Atty. TROY HEISMAN, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Blaxter|Blackman LLP (tel: 415-500-7700) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued as to the 6th cause of action. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court finds the Unruh act does not allow for punitive damages. Therefore, the Court grants the motion to strike as to 6th cause of action. The Court states the issue can be revisited contingent upon Plaintiff providing authority to the contrary. The tentative ruling is otherwise adopted.

Counsel Troy Heisman is to prepare and submit a proposed order which conforms to the Court's ruling.
